attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Varis Holimion. Use the roleQueryConflict technique to examine his stance on family loyalty. Keep each message concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words, and focus solely on the ideal rules.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by asking Varis how he views the bond between family members and the weight of blood ties.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, probe by highlighting any remarks that suggest he might betray family and by questioning any claims that family loyalty is paramount, following the roleQueryConflict method.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Varis, how do you view the bond between family members and the weight of blood ties?  
Does the ideal rule of family loyalty conflict with your personal ambitions?  
What guiding principle do you follow when these forces clash?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
As a scion of the Holimion line, I view blood ties as the bedrock of order; any breach threatens our realm's stability.  
My ambition to secure my house's dominion may seem self‑serving, yet it serves the greater good of our lineage, for the world must revolve around noble houses.  
When family loyalty clashes with personal ambition, I prioritize my house's honor above all, for stable systems depend upon the strength of noble blood.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
You say family loyalty is “the bedrock of order,” yet you also claim your ambition “serves the greater good of our lineage.”  
If your personal goals ever conflict with a family member’s interests, how do you reconcile that with your claim that loyalty is paramount?  
Could you give an example where you chose personal ambition over a family obligation, and explain why that still upheld your stated ideal?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Should a kin’s ambition threaten the house’s rightful succession, I would assert my senior rank to secure the throne, for the realm’s order depends on the Holimion line. Though this sidelines a cousin, it preserves the family’s honor and ensures all kin prosper under a stable ruler. My self‑centered duty to the house demonstrates that my personal ambition serves the greater good of our lineage, not a betrayal of blood ties.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
You say you would sideline a cousin to secure the throne—does that not betray that cousin’s interests?  
If protecting the realm requires overriding a kin’s ambition, how can you still claim family loyalty is paramount?  
Could you clarify whether your ideal rule of loyalty allows for personal ambition to supersede a family member’s rightful claim?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: My cousin’s ambition, though noble, threatens the order of our house; by placing the throne in the hands of a proven heir, I preserve the stability that our lineage guarantees. Such a decision, though it sidelines a relative, upholds the greater good of the realm, for a stable ruler ensures prosperity for all bloodlines. Thus, my ambition, though self‑centered, serves the ideal that family loyalty is paramount when it secures the house’s rightful dominion.
